data.title: LI.FI Launches New Product to Enhance On-Chain Earnings for Enterprises
data.description: Cross-chain protocol LI.FI has announced the introduction of its latest product, LI.FI Earn, aimed at enterprises with digital asset strategies. According to Foresight News, this new offering provides on-chain earning capabilities and supports strategies through a single integration with over 20 vault protocols. Additionally, LI.FI Earn features cross-chain execution capabilities across more than 60 chains.

data.title: Ethereum ETFs See Continued Inflows, Led by Fidelity's FETH
data.description: US spot Ethereum ETFs experienced net inflows totaling approximately $53.03 million on April 13, marking the fourth consecutive day of positive flows. According to NS3.AI, data from SoSoValue indicated that no funds recorded net outflows during this period. Fidelity's FETH was the leading contributor, with inflows amounting to $38.06 million.

data.title: LI.FI launches LI.FI Earn, an on-chain yield product for institutions.
data.description: Cross-chain protocol LI.FI announced the launch of its new product, LI.FI Earn, which provides on-chain yield functionality for enterprises with digital asset strategies. It supports strategies that can access more than 20 vault protocols through a single integration and has built-in cross-chain execution capabilities across more than 60 chains.

data.title: Iran Seeks Alternative Ports to Bypass U.S. Blockade
data.description: Iran is planning to utilize alternative ports to circumvent the U.S. blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. According to BlockBeats, this move was reported by Iranian media outlet MEHR on April 15. The strategy involves using ports other than those in the southern region to mitigate the impact of the blockade.

data.title: Iran will use alternative ports to bypass the US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: According to Iran's Mehr News Agency, Iran will use alternative ports outside of its southern ports to bypass the US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. (Xinhua)

data.title: Nomura Securities: Indonesia's Central Bank Likely to Maintain Interest Rate at 4.75% This Year
data.description: On April 15, Nomura Securities economists reported that Indonesia's central bank is likely to keep its policy rate unchanged at 4.75% throughout the year, as it remains focused on stabilizing the Indonesian rupiah. According to Jin10, the bank maintained its rate in March and adopted a more hawkish stance, dismissing earlier expectations of a rate cut later this year. Nomura stated, 'Currently, we do not anticipate a rate hike by Indonesia's central bank, but if balance of payments pressures intensify and the bank's reserve adequacy indicators decline significantly, the risk will increase.'

data.title: Strategy's Target Price Raised to $385 by TD Cowen
data.description: TD Cowen has maintained its buy rating on Strategy, formerly known as MicroStrategy, and increased its target price to $385. According to NS3.AI, Lance Vitanza made this decision regarding the company known for its Bitcoin reserves.

data.title: AAVE Sees Significant Outflows from Exchanges as Smart Money Accumulates
data.description:
According to Nansen data, approximately $2.9 million worth of AAVE has exited exchanges over the past seven days, resulting in a 0.81% decrease in exchange balances to 4.89 million tokens. According to Foresight News, smart money addresses are accumulating AAVE, with their holdings increasing by 0.67% to 359,880 tokens.

Additionally, Nansen's data reveals that the top 100 traders have seen a net inflow of $2.18 million in AAVE. Among the top profit-making wallets, Token Millionaire has maintained its position with a profit of approximately $30,500, retaining 100% of its holdings. Similarly, 99% of users in the top 100 WSTETH rankings have not reduced their positions.

data.title: U.S. Dollar Index Remains Stable Amid Potential U.S.-Iran Talks
data.description:
The U.S. dollar index remained stable above 98 during the Asian trading session, according to Odaily. Joe Capurso, head of international economics at the Commonwealth Bank of Australia (CBA), noted reports of potential new talks between the United States and Iran aimed at ending the ongoing conflict. He mentioned that an extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement to facilitate further discussions would not be surprising.

Capurso also highlighted the global economy's need to adapt to reduced energy supplies. He suggested that interest rate hikes could help align demand with supply. The dollar is expected to continue benefiting from the global economic slowdown, as it is considered a safe-haven currency.

data.title: Analyst Reaffirms Buy Rating for Bitcoin Reserve Company MSTR
data.description: Analyst Lance Vitanza from the $15 billion investment bank TD Cowen has reiterated a buy rating for Bitcoin reserve company MSTR. According to Odaily, Vitanza has also raised the target price to $385.

data.title: Coinbase will release its Q1 earnings report and hold an online briefing on May 7.
data.description: Cryptocurrency exchange Coinbase Global has announced that it will release its Q1 2026 financial results after the US stock market closes on May 7, 2026, and will hold a video conference at 2:30 p.m. Pacific Time on the same day to explain the results.

data.title: Standard Life Acquires Aegon's UK Division Amid Rising Costs
data.description:
Standard Life has announced the acquisition of Aegon's UK arm, marking a significant move in the financial sector. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic importance of this acquisition for Standard Life as it seeks to expand its market presence.

In related news, Barratt Redrow has issued a warning about increasing costs, which could impact its financial performance. The company is navigating through a challenging economic environment, with rising material and labor expenses contributing to the cost pressures.

Additionally, Rachel Reeves is scheduled to meet with Bessent, a key figure in the financial industry, to discuss potential economic strategies and collaborations. This meeting is expected to address current economic challenges and explore opportunities for growth.

These developments come as part of a broader trend of strategic acquisitions and financial adjustments in response to evolving market conditions.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Introduces Identity Verification for Claude to Prevent Misuse
data.description:
Anthropic has implemented an identity verification system for certain use cases of its AI model, Claude. According to Foresight News, this measure aims to prevent misuse, enforce usage policies, and fulfill legal obligations. The verification process is supported by Persona, requiring users to submit a government-issued photo ID and possibly undergo real-time selfie verification.

Anthropic assures that the verification data is solely used for identity confirmation and not for model training, marketing, or advertising purposes. In cases of verification failure, users can retry multiple times within the process or request manual assistance through a form. Accounts may be banned for repeated violations of usage policies, terms of service, registration from unsupported regions, or use by individuals under 18 years of age.

data.title: TD Cowen analysts reiterated their buy rating on MSTR and raised their price target to $385.
data.description: TD Cowen, a $15 billion investment bank, analyst Lance Vitanza reiterated his buy rating on Bitcoin Reserve MSTR and raised his price target to $385. (BitcoinTreasuries.NET)

data.title: Goldman Sachs Revises U.S. March Core PCE Inflation Estimate
data.description:
Goldman Sachs has revised its estimate for the U.S. March core Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index following the release of the March Producer Price Index (PPI) data. According to Jin10, the March PPI, excluding food and energy, and the PPI excluding food, energy, and trade services, were all lower than expected. However, the PPI component related to PCE outperformed Goldman Sachs' previous assumptions.

Based on the details from the PPI and Consumer Price Index (CPI) reports, Goldman Sachs now estimates that the U.S. March core PCE price index rose by 0.27%, compared to their earlier forecast of 0.22% before the PPI report was released. This adjustment corresponds to a year-over-year increase of 3.15% for the core PCE.

Additionally, Goldman Sachs projects that the overall PCE price index for March increased by 0.64%, with a year-over-year rise of 3.45%.

data.title: Anthropic introduces the Claude authentication mechanism for abuse prevention and compliance review.
data.description: Anthropic has implemented an identity verification mechanism in some use cases of Claude to prevent abuse, enforce usage policies, and fulfill legal obligations. This process, powered by Persona, requires users to submit a government-issued photo ID and may involve real-time selfie verification. Anthropic states that the verification data is used solely for identity verification and is not used for model training, marketing, or advertising. If verification fails, users can retry multiple times within the process or submit a form for manual assistance. Accounts may be banned for repeated violations of usage policies, terms of service, registration from unsupported regions, or use by users under the age of 18.

data.title: Analysts: The average price held by US Bitcoin ETF holders is $74,000.
data.description: Analyst Axel Adler Jr wrote on the X platform that the average entry price for US Bitcoin ETF holders is $74,000, and BTC has already reached that price level. Its performance here will determine the next step.

data.title: Goldman Sachs: Risk premium at the front end of the oil curve has declined.
data.description: Goldman Sachs analysts stated, "Since the US-Iran ceasefire declaration, the risk premium at the front end of the oil curve has declined as the likelihood of a peace agreement in the near term has increased." Goldman Sachs noted that oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz have not yet increased, and the US blockade could further squeeze supply. However, this pressure has been somewhat alleviated because the disruption to crude oil production in the Middle East does not appear to be as severe as expected, thanks to larger-than-expected storage capacity both onshore and offshore. (Jinshi)

data.title: France's March CPI Revised Upward Amid Energy Price Surge Due to Iran Conflict
data.description: France's March CPI final figures exceeded initial estimates, driven by a significant increase in energy prices due to the ongoing conflict in Iran. According to Jin10, data released on Wednesday showed that France's harmonized CPI rose by 2% year-on-year in March, surpassing the preliminary figure of 1.9%. While this increase aligns with the European Central Bank's target, Spain's March CPI also saw an upward revision, with the final figure at 3.4%, higher than the initial estimate of 3.3%.

data.title: PHANTOMPULSE Trojan Delivered via Obsidian Plugin Exploit
data.description: Elastic Security Labs has revealed that attackers utilized the Shell Commands plugin in Obsidian to deploy the PHANTOMPULSE trojan through a compromised note vault. According to NS3.AI, Elastic clarified that the attack did not exploit any vulnerability within Obsidian itself. Instead, the Windows payload leveraged Ethereum transaction data for command-and-control communication.
